title: quercus elmeri
intro: Quercus elmeri, also known as Elmer's oak, is a species of deciduous tree in the family Fagaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Philippines and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus elmeri include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Quercus elmeri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
